thesis_anchors_verified: Per chapter-2 thesis: 'duty is not optional, even when it is hard' — 2.34 names the consequence of the abandonment in reputation-as-imperishable-signal terms.
Per chapter-2 thesis: 'didactic, corrective, slightly stern' — 2.34 voice is appropriately sharp; signal-theory framing without softening.

Per chapter-2 thesis: imperishable substrate of 2.11-2.30 — 2.34 deliberately mirrors 'avyaya' from 2.17 to make the structural point about manufactured imperishable signal. The engineering layer surfaces this mirror, which is doctrinally important and chapter-coherent.
Per chapter-2 thesis: counter-anti-thesis 'It is not teaching that operators should not feel the weight of consequences' — 2.34 carries this, naming the consequence directly.
ordering_check: 2.34 correctly follows 2.33. 2.33 named the if-then loss-trio (svadharma, kīrti, pāpa); 2.34 develops the kīrti-loss alone into avyayām akīrtim. 2.35 then sharpens to mahā-rathas as the specific witness class. The block has clear progressive escalation: condition (2.33) → universal-scope reputation cost (2.34) → peer-class-specific reputation cost (2.35).
verdict_reason: Verse supports thesis directly and is correctly positioned. PASS on both 10.3 and 10.4.
